criteria.description: Severity levels are color-coded when outputting to a TTY
criteria.evidence: FormatTextColor is never called from any production code. StreamEntry (used by search.go:136) calls FormatText which has no color support. There is no TTY detection code in the project. FormatTextColor is dead code exercised only by tests.

criteria.description: Colors are disabled automatically when stdout is not a TTY
criteria.evidence: No TTY detection exists anywhere in the codebase. No os.Stdout.Stat(), no ModeCharDevice check, no isatty equivalent. The colorEnabled bool parameter in FormatTextColor is never set by any caller based on terminal state.

failureReason: FormatTextColor is dead code: it is never integrated into the CLI output path. StreamEntry calls FormatText (no color). No TTY detection exists. Both criteria fail at the integration level despite unit tests passing.
